authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Eldorado National Forest (Desolation Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Maud Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Maud Lake not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Aquatic Biodiversity Management Plan for the Desolation Wilderness — California Department of Fish and Wildlife (12/19/2012)
sources.quotedText: Maud Lake is a popular overnight destination accessed via the well-used Rockbound Pass Trail. It was stocked by CDFG with rainbow trout until 2000. CDFG gill net samples in 2002 and 2010 indicate rainbow trout and brown trout are present in the lake and self-sustaining. As a result, the lake will be managed as a self-sustaining fishery.

sources.title: Desolation Wilderness Permits — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use. ... HIGH USE SEASON A quota in place from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 of each year.

parameters.note: Maud Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1) (open all year). Although in the Lake Tahoe Basin region, it is NOT a Lake Tahoe tributary: CDFW records place it in the Jones Fork Silver Creek planning watershed, which drains west of the Sierra crest to the South Fork American River. The §7.50(b)(152) Tahoe-tributary catch-and-release closure therefore does not apply. GNIS feature ID 228241 ('Maude Lake', the USGS official spelling for this waterbody, El Dorado Co., CA) resolved from the authoritative USGS Geographic Names Information System.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water and not a Lake Tahoe tributary)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit. Applies to both the rainbow and brown trout present in the lake (combined as 'trout').

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Fishery status: Maud Lake was stocked by CDFW with rainbow trout until 2000; stocking then ceased (Do Not Plant). CDFW gill-net samples in 2002 and 2010 found both rainbow trout and brown trout present and self-sustaining, so the lake is now managed as a self-sustaining fishery — it is NOT currently stocked, but supports naturally reproducing wild trout. This is a fishery-management status, not a take restriction.

regulations.citation: CDFW Aquatic Biodiversity Management Plan for the Desolation Wilderness (2012) — Jones Fork Silver Creek PWS
regulations.humanSummary: Self-sustaining wild fishery: stocking ceased in 2000, but CDFW 2002 and 2010 gill-net samples confirm self-sustaining rainbow trout and brown trout — the lake is managed as a self-sustaining fishery and is no longer planted.
regulations.verbatimText: CDFG gill net samples in 2002 and 2010 indicate rainbow trout and brown trout are present in the lake and self-sustaining. As a result, the lake will be managed as a self-sustaining fishery.

parameters.description: Maud Lake lies within the Desolation Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest), a popular overnight destination on the Rockbound Pass Trail. A USDA Forest Service wilderness permit is required for overnight use year-round; during the high-use quota season (Friday before Memorial Day through September 30) overnight visits are subject to a zone quota and day-use permits are required at the trailheads.

raw.quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USFS Eldorado National Forest — Desolation Wilderness Permits
regulations.humanSummary: Access is inside the Desolation Wilderness: a Forest Service wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use, with an overnight zone quota in effect from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30.
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.
